name: White chocolate and raspberry

ingredients.original: 6 tsp raspberry jam, store-bought or homemade

method: Crush the fresh raspberries with a fork and then pass them through a fine sieve to remove the seeds. Set the smooth coulis aside.

Put the white chocolate and the butter in a heatproof bowl. Heat the cream in a small saucepan and bring to a boil, watching it constantly. As soon as it comes to a boil, pour it over the chocolate and butter and stir gently with a rubber spatula. Continue until all the chocolate has melted and you are left with a smooth, shiny ganache.

Immediately, before the ganache begins to set, spoon 1 teaspoon jam into each shell. Carefully pour in the ganache; it should almost reach to the rim (if the ganache does begin to set, heat it gently over a pan of hot water before pouring). Be very gentle now and don’t shake the shells.

Spoon a tiny amount of the raspberry coulis—not more than ½ teaspoon—into the center of each tartlet. Use the tip of a knife or a skewer to swirl the coulis around. Carefully transfer the tartlets to the fridge and leave them there to set. Remove at least 30 minutes before serving.
